name: Louisiana Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Louisiana Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

address.streetAddress: 18550 Highland Road, Suite B, Baton Rouge, LA 70809
address.description:
LBESPA

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Louisiana SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: $125 initial. $75 annual renewal (Louisiana requires annual renewal).

mainEntity.name: Is Louisiana part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Louisiana is one of the first states actively issuing compact privileges as of October 2025.

mainEntity.name: Does Louisiana allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Louisiana have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Louisiana participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Louisiana without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Louisiana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
